heading: Assignment of right, title, and interest in the Union Station complex to the Secretary of Transportation
label: 6902 Assignment of right, title, and interest in the Union Station complex to the Secretary of Transportation
text: The Secretary of Transportation has the right, title, and interest in and to the Union Station complex, including all agreements and leases made under sections 101–110 of the National Visitors Center Facilities Act of 1968 (, ). To the extent the Secretary of Transportation and the Secretary of the Interior agree, the Secretary of the Interior may lease space for visitor services.Public Law 90–26482 Stat. 43

References in TextSections 101–110 of the National Visitors Center Facilities Act of 1968, referred to in text, are sections 101 to 110 of , , , which were classified principally to part A (§ 801 et seq.) of subchapter I of chapter 18 of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, prior to repeal by , , . Section 104 of the Act was classified as a note under section 804 of former Title 40 prior to repeal by . Section 108 of the Act was not classified to the Code.Pub.
